summary: Fix two CLHS conformance bugs in src/core/builtins_io.c: LOAD must honor :if-does-not-exist (and the other standard keyword args), and COMPILE-FILE must not evaluate ordinary top-level forms at compile time.

items.title: LOAD: parse keyword args; return NIL on missing file when :if-does-not-exist is NIL
items.description:
Bug B from the request: `(load "/tmp/nope.lisp" :if-does-not-exist nil)` must return NIL, but bi_load currently has `CL_UNUSED(n)` and only reads `args[0]` — every keyword is dropped on the floor, so any missing file unconditionally signals via cl_error.

Location: src/core/builtins_io.c, function `bi_load` (defined at src/core/builtins_io.c:373) and its registration at src/core/builtins_io.c:3213.

Implementation:

1. At the top of `bi_load` (immediately after the `CL_UNUSED(n);` line at src/core/builtins_io.c:390), parse the standard LOAD keyword args. Use the same loop shape already used by bi_compile_file (src/core/builtins_io.c:1195-1210) — `for (i = 1; i + 1 < n; i += 2)` with `cl_intern_keyword("NAME", len)` comparisons. Collect into locals:
   - `int verbose_explicit = 0;` and `int verbose = 0;`
   - `int print_explicit  = 0;` and `int print  = 0;`
   - `CL_Obj if_dne = CL_UNBOUND;` — unbound sentinel so we can tell "not supplied" (CLHS default is T → signal an error) from "supplied as NIL" (return NIL) from "supplied as T" (signal).
   - Accept and ignore (for now) :external-format. Treat unknown kwargs as allow-other-keys (consistent with bi_compile_file).
   Remove `CL_UNUSED(n);` since `n` is now used.

2. After parsing, fold the explicit values into the existing flow:
   - Replace the `*load-verbose*` check at src/core/builtins_io.c:610-614 with: if `verbose_explicit` use `verbose`, else use `!CL_NULL_P(cl_symbol_value(SYM_STAR_LOAD_VERBOSE))`. Do the same for the verbose check on the FASL-cache path at src/core/builtins_io.c:511-516 so the cached path and the source path agree on the flag.
   - For :print, add a single per-top-level-form print after `cl_vm_eval(bytecode)` in the source loop (src/core/builtins_io.c:738): when print is true, write the last value (the value left at `cl_vm.stack[cl_vm.sp - 1]` or via `cl_mv_values[0]` as appropriate — match the convention used by repl.c). If implementing :print is non-trivial, this can be a minimal stub (parse the keyword but ignore the value) — the registration comment at src/core/builtins_io.c:3213 already claims support; do not regress what's already there.

3. Handle :if-does-not-exist around the file-read at src/core/builtins_io.c:577:
   - Move the platform_file_read of the source path so its result is checked against `if_dne` BEFORE the `cl_error` call.
   - On NULL return:
     - If `if_dne` is CL_NIL (explicitly supplied NIL): restore `cl_current_package` / *package* / source-file context (none of the per-file bindings have been set yet at this point; just match the early-exit cleanup) and return CL_NIL.
     - Otherwise (unbound = default, or supplied T, or any other non-NIL): cl_error as today, but with a clearer message: `cl_error(CL_ERR_FILE, "LOAD: file does not exist: %s", path_str->data)` — include the path so the user sees which file failed (currently the message is just "LOAD: cannot open file"). Reuse CL_ERR_GENERAL if CL_ERR_FILE is not defined.
   - The same `:if-does-not-exist NIL → return NIL` check must ALSO run BEFORE the FASL-cache lookup (src/core/builtins_io.c:449-575). The cache lookup is purely an optimisation; if the source file is missing AND the caller is OK with that, we must not silently substitute a stale cached FASL. Cheapest fix: do a `platform_file_exists(path_str->data)` check immediately after kwarg parsing and short-circuit to NIL (or to the existing cl_error) before entering the cache block. The existing `platform_file_mtime` comparison at src/core/builtins_io.c:459 already needs the source file to exist to be meaningful.

4. Update the registration comment at src/core/builtins_io.c:3213 to: `defun("LOAD", bi_load, 1, -1);  /* &key :verbose :print :if-does-not-exist :external-format */`.

5. Regression test — add to tests/test_fasl_compat.sh as a new check just before the cleanup block (around tests/test_fasl_compat.sh:260), or create tests/test_load_keywords.sh and register it in the Makefile test section (mirror the test_load_exit.sh entry at Makefile:129-134):
   - `(load "/nonexistent/path.lisp" :if-does-not-exist nil)` exits 0 and prints NIL (no error).
   - `(load "/nonexistent/path.lisp")` (default :if-does-not-exist T) exits non-zero with an error message that includes the path.
   - `(load "/nonexistent/path.lisp" :if-does-not-exist t)` also errors (same as default).
   - When the file DOES exist, :if-does-not-exist nil has no effect (loads normally, returns T).
   Use the existing `run_quiet` helper / pattern.

6. Do NOT bump CL_FASL_VERSION — this change has no effect on on-disk FASL layout.

7. Run `make test` from /workspace; it must pass. Spot-check that ASDF / quicklisp paths (which call (load ... :if-does-not-exist nil) heavily) continue to work — `./build/host/clamiga --heap 24M --load trunk/load-and-test-fset.lisp` is a quick smoke test of the ASDF code path.

items.title: COMPILE-FILE: stop evaluating ordinary top-level forms at compile time (CLHS 3.2.3.1)
items.description:
Bug A from the request: bi_compile_file's per-form driver (`cf_process_toplevel_form` at src/core/builtins_io.c:1063) calls `cl_vm_eval(bytecode)` on EVERY top-level form. Per CLHS 3.2.3.1, the only forms whose side effects may run at compile time are (a) defining-macro forms whose own semantics require it (DEFMACRO, DEFTYPE, DEFCONSTANT, DEFSETF, IN-PACKAGE), and (b) the body of `(eval-when (:compile-toplevel) ...)`. For everything else, compile-file must serialize the bytecode for load-time only. The concrete bug: `(defvar *p* (make-package :pkgtest-foo))` runs `make-package` during compile-file, then again during load — the second call signals "Package already exists".

Location: src/core/builtins_io.c, function `cf_process_toplevel_form` at src/core/builtins_io.c:1063-1094. The function currently does, for every cons form: cl_compile → cl_vm_eval → cf_bc_vec_append. The fix is to replace that with a CLHS-Table-3-9-aware dispatch.

Implementation:

1. Replace the body of `cf_process_toplevel_form` with the following structure (keeping the existing PROGN-recursion and atom-skip behavior):

   ```c
   /* Top-level macroexpand-1 loop: per CLHS 3.2.3.1, a top-level macro
    * form is processed as the macro expansion would be (e.g. DEFPACKAGE
    * expands to an (eval-when ...) wrapper, which we then handle below). */
   for (;;) {
       CL_Obj exp;
       if (!CL_CONS_P(expr)) return;  /* atom: nothing to do */
       exp = cl_macroexpand_1_env(expr, CL_NIL);
       if (exp == expr) break;
       expr = exp;
   }

   if (cl_car(expr) == SYM_PROGN) { /* existing recursion */ ... return; }
   if (cl_car(expr) == SYM_EVAL_WHEN) { cf_process_toplevel_eval_when(expr, bc_vec_p, bc_count_p); return; }

   /* Compile to bytecode. Per CLHS 3.2.3.1.2, the compile-time effects of
    * defining forms (proclaim-special for DEFVAR/DEFPARAMETER, mark-constant
    * for DEFCONSTANT, install class/struct/method-combination metadata, etc.)
    * are produced AS PART OF cl_compile via compile_defvar / compile_defparameter /
    * compile_defconstant — so the symbol-table side effect is already in place
    * by the time we get the bytecode back. We only need to run the bytecode
    * at compile time for the small set of forms whose compile-time effect is
    * produced by OP_DEFMACRO / OP_DEFSETF / OP_DEFTYPE etc. running, not by
    * the compile pass itself. */
   bytecode = cl_compile(expr);
   if (CL_NULL_P(bytecode)) return;
   CL_GC_PROTECT(bytecode);
   if (cf_form_needs_compile_time_eval(expr))
       cl_vm_eval(bytecode);
   cf_bc_vec_append(bc_vec_p, bc_count_p, bytecode);
   cl_gc_compact_if_pending();
   CL_GC_UNPROTECT(1);
   ```

2. Add helper `cf_form_needs_compile_time_eval(CL_Obj expr)` returning 1 when the form's head (after the macroexpand loop above) requires compile-time evaluation for subsequent forms in the same compilation unit to compile correctly. Initial set:
   - SYM_DEFMACRO  — later forms must macroexpand using this macro
   - SYM_DEFTYPE   — later (typep), (check-type), (the) calls need the type
   - SYM_DEFCONSTANT — later forms refer to the constant value
   - SYM_DEFSETF   — later (setf …) must see the expander
   - SYM_IN_PACKAGE — affects how subsequent forms read (compile_in_package already updates cl_current_package at compile time, but the bytecode also stores into *PACKAGE*; running it keeps the Lisp special var in sync)

   Notably ABSENT from the list (so they no longer run at compile time):
   - SYM_DEFVAR, SYM_DEFPARAMETER — proclaim-special is already done by compile_defvar/compile_defparameter at compile time (src/core/compiler_extra.c:1090, src/core/compiler_extra.c:1111). The initform must NOT run until load.
   - SYM_DEFUN — CLHS allows the compiler to record the name/arglist (and clamiga's compile_defun already does whatever it needs); the body must not run at compile time.
   - DEFPACKAGE / DEFCLASS / DEFGENERIC / DEFSTRUCT / DEFINE-CONDITION / DEFINE-METHOD-COMBINATION / DEFINE-COMPILER-MACRO / DEFINE-MODIFY-MACRO / DEFINE-SETF-EXPANDER / DEFINE-SYMBOL-MACRO — these are user-level macros (see lib/boot.lisp:946 for DEFPACKAGE, lib/clos.lisp etc.) that expand into `(eval-when (:compile-toplevel :load-toplevel :execute) …)`. The top-level macroexpand-1 loop above unwraps them so they hit the EVAL-WHEN branch.

3. Add helper `cf_process_toplevel_eval_when(CL_Obj form, CL_Obj *bc_vec_p, int *bc_count_p)` implementing CLHS Table 3-9:
   - Pull `situations = cl_car(cl_cdr(form))` and `body = cl_cdr(cl_cdr(form))`.
   - Walk `situations` testing each element against the keyword AND legacy-symbol forms (both forms are valid per CLHS): `:compile-toplevel` or `compile`, `:load-toplevel` or `load`, `:execute` or `eval`. Intern these once and cache in statics at the top of builtins_io.c near the other KW_* statics, e.g. `KW_EW_CT = cl_intern_keyword("COMPILE-TOPLEVEL", 16)`, plus the CL-package legacy names. Set three flags `has_ct`, `has_lt`, `has_ex`.
   - Apply Table 3-9 to the body, treating each body subform as a fresh top-level form (recurse via the existing PROGN-recursion helper or by calling cf_process_toplevel_form on each subform):
     - has_ct && has_lt:  for each subform → process as toplevel (the recursive call will eval defining forms and collect everything)
     - has_ct && !has_lt: for each subform → cl_compile + cl_vm_eval; do NOT collect (no FASL emission)
     - !has_ct && has_lt: for each subform → cl_compile + cf_bc_vec_append; do NOT eval at compile time
     - !has_ct && !has_lt && has_ex: "not at top level" mode per CLHS — compile + eval, do NOT collect. (Equivalent to wrapping the body in PROGN and running it now, since :execute alone means "act as if not at toplevel".)
     - otherwise (no situations active): do nothing.
   - Implementation tip: factor the recursive subform processing into a small helper so the four branches stay readable.

4. Reference the standard via comments in `cf_process_toplevel_form` and the new helpers — cite CLHS 3.2.3.1 and Table 3-9 so the next reader can verify the rules against the spec.

5. The existing `compile_eval_when` in src/core/compiler_extra.c:940 is reached only for NON-top-level EVAL-WHEN forms (or when a top-level eval-when has has_lt — i.e. its body's bytecode is being collected for FASL). Its existing behavior (always execute body) is correct for non-toplevel per CLHS 3.2.3.1.2 (only :execute matters there). Do not change it unless tests force a fix.

6. Regression tests — add to tests/test_fasl_compat.sh (in the same style as the existing tests there). Group as a new section before the cleanup. Each test should clean its own /tmp/ file at the end. All tests must run in a SINGLE clamiga process (compile-file + load same session):

   - `compile_file_does_not_eval_defvar_initform`: write a file containing `(defvar *cf-side-effect* (progn (push :compiled *cf-trace*) :v))` plus `(defvar *cf-trace* nil)` (note: trace first so initform can push), then `(compile-file …)` followed by `(load …)`. After loading, `*cf-trace*` must equal `(:compiled)` (not `(:compiled :compiled)`). Use a clean cache to ensure compile-file actually runs.
   - `compile_file_does_not_run_make_package_twice`: exact repro from the request — file = `(defvar *p* (make-package :clamiga-bug-a-test))`; in one process do compile-file then load; the load must NOT signal "Package already exists". Verify by checking the process exits 0 and `(find-package :clamiga-bug-a-test)` is non-NIL.
   - `compile_file_eval_when_compile_toplevel_runs`: file = `(eval-when (:compile-toplevel) (defparameter *ew-ct* 1))` plus `(defparameter *ew-runtime-check* (boundp '*ew-ct*)))`. After compile-file (in a fresh process — `*ew-ct*` should be bound during compilation only). Or: assert that the print/format inside the eval-when fires during the compile-file invocation (check via captured stdout).
   - `compile_file_eval_when_load_toplevel_does_not_run_at_compile`: file = `(eval-when (:load-toplevel) (format t "LOAD-ONLY~%"))`. Capture stdout of just `(compile-file …)`; assert it does NOT contain "LOAD-ONLY". Capture stdout of subsequent `(load …)`; assert it DOES contain "LOAD-ONLY".
   - `compile_file_defmacro_available_for_later_forms`: file = `(defmacro cf-mac (x) `(list :wrapped ,x)) (defparameter *cf-mac-r* (cf-mac 7))`. After compile-file + load, `*cf-mac-r*` must equal `(:wrapped 7)`. Verifies the defining-form whitelist still works.
   - `compile_file_does_not_run_top_level_call_twice`: file = `(defparameter *cf-counter* 0) (incf *cf-counter*)`. After compile-file then load in the same process, `*cf-counter*` must equal 1, not 2. Direct regression on "non-defining top-level forms must not run at compile time".

   For the negative-output tests (compile-file should NOT print …), use `--non-interactive --no-userinit` and capture stdout/stderr; `grep -v` style assertions are fine — see test_fasl_compat.sh's existing `check_contains` and add a `check_not_contains` helper if needed.

7. Also add a mirror of `compile_file_does_not_run_make_package_twice` to tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp (alongside other compile-file checks; search for `compile-file` in that file to find the right section). Cross-platform regression coverage per CLAUDE.md's "every new feature or bugfix must have both host and Amiga tests".

8. CL_FASL_VERSION: this change does NOT modify the FASL byte layout. The set of bytecodes serialized changes (we now emit bytecode for forms whose effect previously happened at compile time only — DEFUN, top-level calls, etc. — but those bytecodes were also being emitted before; we just skipped re-running them at load time). The bytecode encoding is unchanged. Do NOT bump CL_FASL_VERSION. If the cf_bc_vec_append output diverges in a way the test suite or a load of a cached FASL detects, re-evaluate.

9. Performance / GC note: removing `cl_vm_eval` for the common case may change GC root anchoring — `bytecode` is still GC-protected across `cf_bc_vec_append` per the existing code (src/core/builtins_io.c:1089-1093), and the function pointers inside the bytecode object remain rooted via the vector. No new protection points should be needed, but cross-check by running the heavyweight tests: `make test-extra` (in particular trunk/load-and-test-fset.lisp and trunk/load-and-test-5am.lisp) to confirm no GC regressions under load.

10. Run `make test` (mandatory) and `make test-plus`. Also run `./build/host/clamiga --heap 24M --load trunk/load-and-test-5am.lisp` and `./build/host/clamiga --heap 24M --load trunk/load-and-test-fset.lisp` as smoke tests for ASDF + Quicklisp compile-file paths — both already exercise compile-file heavily and would surface any regression in defining-form handling. Optional but recommended: cross-compile via `make -f Makefile.cross test-amiga` to verify Amiga remains green.
